title: Interface getter functions for interface parameters return default (not overridden) value

features.name: interface parameter hierarchical access in constant expression
features.description: Code should include an interface with a parameter that is instantiated with an override and accessed via hierarchical reference from a module, where the parameter is used in a constant expression
features.snippet:
interface test_if #(parameter integer FOO = 1); endinterface
module m; test_if #(.FOO(5)) i(); localparam P = i.FOO; endmodule

features.name: interface parameter accessed via modport import function
features.description: Code should include an interface with a modport that imports a function returning the interface parameter, instantiated with an override, where the function result is used in a constant expression
features.snippet:
interface test_if #(parameter integer FOO = 1); modport mp(import getFoo); function integer getFoo(); return FOO; endfunction endinterface
module m; test_if #(.FOO(5)) i(); test_if.mp intf = i; localparam P = intf.getFoo(); endmodule

features.name: interface parameterized function called from outside in constant context
features.description: Code should include an interface with a function that returns a parameter value, instantiated with a parameter override, accessed via hierarchical function call from a module in a constant expression
features.snippet:
interface test_if #(parameter integer FOO = 1); function integer getFoo(); return FOO; endfunction endinterface
module m; test_if #(.FOO(5)) i(); localparam P = i.getFoo(); endmodule
